NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. Plead my cause, O LORD, with those who strive with me; Fight against those who fight against me.
NKJV.2: Take hold of shield and buckler, And stand up for my help.
NKJV.3: Also draw out the spear, And stop those who pursue me. Say to my soul, “I am your salvation.”
NKJV.4: Let those be put to shame and brought to dishonor Who seek after my life; Let those be turned back and brought to confusion Who plot my hurt.
NKJV.5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, And let the angel of the LORD chase them.
NKJV.6: Let their way be dark and slippery, And let the angel of the LORD pursue them.
NKJV.7: For without cause they have hidden their net for me in a pit, Which they have dug without cause for my life.
NKJV.8: Let destruction come upon him unexpectedly, And let his net that he has hidden catch himself; Into that very destruction let him fall.
NKJV.9: And my soul shall be joyful in the LORD; It shall rejoice in His salvation.
NKJV.10: All my bones shall say, “LORD, who is like You, Delivering the poor from him who is too strong for him, Yes, the poor and the needy from him who plunders him?”
NKJV.11: Fierce witnesses rise up; They ask me things that I do not know.
NKJV.12: They reward me evil for good, To the sorrow of my soul.
NKJV.13: But as for me, when they were sick, My clothing was sackcloth; I humbled myself with fasting; And my prayer would return to my own heart.
NKJV.14: I paced about as though he were my friend or brother; I bowed down heavily, as one who mourns for his mother.
NKJV.15: But in my adversity they rejoiced And gathered together; Attackers gathered against me, And I did not know it; They tore at me and did not cease;
NKJV.16: With ungodly mockers at feasts They gnashed at me with their teeth.
NKJV.17: Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue me from their destructions, My precious life from the lions.
NKJV.18: I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.
NKJV.19: Let them not rejoice over me who are wrongfully my enemies; Nor let them wink with the eye who hate me without a cause.
NKJV.20: For they do not speak peace, But they devise deceitful matters Against the quiet ones in the land.
NKJV.21: They also opened their mouth wide against me, And said, “Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it.”
NKJV.22: This You have seen, O LORD; Do not keep silence. O Lord, do not be far from me.
NKJV.23: Stir up Yourself, and awake to my vindication, To my cause, my God and my Lord.
NKJV.24: Vindicate me, O LORD my God, according to Your righteousness; And let them not rejoice over me.
NKJV.25: Let them not say in their hearts, “Ah, so we would have it!” Let them not say, “We have swallowed him up.”
NKJV.26: Let them be ashamed and brought to mutual confusion Who rejoice at my hurt; Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor Who exalt themselves against me.
NKJV.27: Let them shout for joy and be glad, Who favor my righteous cause; And let them say continually, “Let the LORD be magnified, Who has pleasure in the prosperity of His servant.”
NKJV.28: And my tongue shall speak of Your righteousness And of Your praise all the day long.

KJV.4: Let them be confounded<S>954</S> and put to shame<S>3637</S> that seek<S>1245</S> after my soul<S>5315</S>: let them be turned<S>5472</S> back<S>268</S> and brought to confusion<S>2659</S> that devise<S>2803</S> my hurt<S>7451</S>.
KJV.5: Let them be as chaff<S>4671</S> before<S>6440</S> the wind<S>7307</S>: and let the angel<S>4397</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> chase<S>1760</S> them.

KJV.7: For without cause<S>2600</S> have they hid<S>2934</S> for me their net<S>7568</S> in a pit<S>7845</S>, which without cause<S>2600</S> they have digged<S>2658</S> for my soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.8: Let destruction<S>7722</S> come<S>935</S> upon him at unawares<S>3045</S> <S>3808</S>; and let his net<S>7568</S> that he hath hid<S>2934</S> catch<S>3920</S> himself: into that very destruction<S>7722</S> let him fall<S>5307</S>. <sup>at: Heb. which he knoweth not of</sup>
KJV.9: And my soul<S>5315</S> shall be joyful<S>1523</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>: it shall rejoice<S>7797</S> in his salvation<S>3444</S>.

KJV.11: False<S>2555</S> witnesses<S>5707</S> did rise up<S>6965</S>; they laid to my charge<S>7592</S> things that I knew<S>3045</S> not. <sup>False: Heb. Witnesses of wrong</sup> <sup>they: Heb. they asked me</sup>

KJV.13: But as for me, when they were sick<S>2470</S>, my clothing<S>3830</S> was sackcloth<S>8242</S>: I humbled<S>6031</S> my soul<S>5315</S> with fasting<S>6685</S>; and my prayer<S>8605</S> returned<S>7725</S> into mine own bosom<S>2436</S>. <sup>humbled: or, afflicted</sup>

ESV.1: Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me; fight against those who fight against me!
ESV.2: Take hold of shield and buckler and rise for my help!
ESV.3: Draw the spear and javelin  against my pursuers! Say to my soul, “I am your salvation!”
ESV.4: Let them be put to shame and dishonor who seek after my life! Let them be turned back and disappointed who devise evil against me!
ESV.5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them away!
ESV.6: Let their way be dark and slippery, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them!
ESV.7: For without cause they hid their net for me; without cause they dug a pit for my life.
ESV.8: Let destruction come upon him when he does not know it! And let the net that he hid ensnare him; let him fall into it — to his destruction!
ESV.9: Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD, exulting in his salvation.
ESV.10: All my bones shall say, “O LORD, who is like you, delivering the poor from him who is too strong for him, the poor and needy from him who robs him?”
ESV.11: Malicious  witnesses rise up; they ask me of things that I do not know.
ESV.12: They repay me evil for good; my soul is bereft.
ESV.13: But I, when they were sick — I wore sackcloth; I afflicted myself with fasting; I prayed with head bowed  on my chest.
ESV.14: I went about as though I grieved for my friend or my brother; as one who laments his mother, I bowed down in mourning.
ESV.15: But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered; they gathered together against me; wretches whom I did not know tore at me without ceasing;
ESV.16: like profane mockers at a feast,  they gnash at me with their teeth.
ESV.17: How long, O Lord, will you look on? Rescue me from their destruction, my precious life from the lions!
ESV.18: I will thank you in the great congregation; in the mighty throng I will praise you.
ESV.19: Let not those rejoice over me who are wrongfully my foes, and let not those wink the eye who hate me without cause.
ESV.20: For they do not speak peace, but against those who are quiet in the land they devise words of deceit.
ESV.21: They open wide their mouths against me; they say, “Aha, Aha! Our eyes have seen it!”
ESV.22: You have seen, O LORD; be not silent! O Lord, be not far from me!
ESV.23: Awake and rouse yourself for my vindication, for my cause, my God and my Lord!
ESV.24: Vindicate me, O LORD, my God, according to your righteousness, and let them not rejoice over me!
ESV.25: Let them not say in their hearts, “Aha, our heart's desire!” Let them not say, “We have swallowed him up.”
ESV.26: Let them be put to shame and disappointed altogether who rejoice at my calamity! Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me!
ESV.27: Let those who delight in my righteousness shout for joy and be glad and say evermore, “Great is the LORD, who delights in the welfare of his servant!”
ESV.28: Then my tongue shall tell of your righteousness and of your praise all the day long.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me; Fight against those who fight against me.
NASB.2: Take hold of buckler and shield And rise up for my help.
NASB.3: Draw also the spear and the battle-axe to meet those who pursue me; Say to my soul, 'I am your salvation.'
NASB.4: Let those be ashamed and dishonored who seek my life; Let those be turned back and humiliated who devise evil against me.
NASB.5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, With the angel of the LORD driving [them] on.
NASB.6: Let their way be dark and slippery, With the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
NASB.7: For without cause they hid their net for me; Without cause they dug a pit for my soul.
NASB.8: Let destruction come upon him unawares, And let the net which he hid catch himself; Into that very destruction let him fall.
NASB.9: And my soul shall rejoice in the LORD; It shall exult in His salvation.
NASB.10: All my bones will say, 'LORD, who is like You, Who delivers the afflicted from him who is too strong for him, And the afflicted and the needy from him who robs him?'
NASB.11: Malicious witnesses rise up; They ask me of things that I do not know.
NASB.12: They repay me evil for good, [To] the bereavement of my soul.
NASB.13: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth; I humbled my soul with fasting, And my prayer kept returning to my bosom.
NASB.14: I went about as though it were my friend or brother; I bowed down mourning, as one who sorrows for a mother.
NASB.15: But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered themselves together; The smiters whom I did not know gathered together against me, They slandered me without ceasing.
NASB.16: Like godless jesters at a feast, They gnashed at me with their teeth.
NASB.17: Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue my soul from their ravages, My only [life] from the lions.
NASB.18: I will give You thanks in the great congregation; I will praise You among a mighty throng.
NASB.19: Do not let those who are wrongfully my enemies rejoice over me; Nor let those who hate me without cause wink maliciously.
NASB.20: For they do not speak peace, But they devise deceitful words against those who are quiet in the land.
NASB.21: They opened their mouth wide against me; They said, 'Aha, aha, our eyes have seen it!'
NASB.22: You have seen it, O LORD, do not keep silent; O Lord, do not be far from me.
NASB.23: Stir up Yourself, and awake to my right And to my cause, my God and my Lord.
NASB.24: Judge me, O LORD my God, according to Your righteousness, And do not let them rejoice over me.
NASB.25: Do not let them say in their heart, 'Aha, our desire!' Do not let them say, 'We have swallowed him up!'
NASB.26: Let those be ashamed and humiliated altogether who rejoice at my distress; Let those be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves over me.
NASB.27: Let them shout for joy and rejoice, who favor my vindication; And let them say continually, 'The LORD be magnified, Who delights in the prosperity of His servant.'
NASB.28: And my tongue shall declare Your righteousness [And] Your praise all day long.

NLT.1:
O LORD, oppose those who oppose me.
Fight those who fight against me.
NLT.2:
Put on your armor, and take up your shield.
Prepare for battle, and come to my aid.
NLT.3:
Lift up your spear and javelin
against those who pursue me.
Let me hear you say,
“I will give you victory!”
NLT.4:
Bring shame and disgrace on those trying to kill me;
turn them back and humiliate those who want to harm me.
NLT.5:
Blow them away like chaff in the wind —
a wind sent by the angel of the LORD.
NLT.6:
Make their path dark and slippery,
with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
NLT.7:
I did them no wrong, but they laid a trap for me.
I did them no wrong, but they dug a pit to catch me.
NLT.8:
So let sudden ruin come upon them!
Let them be caught in the trap they set for me!
Let them be destroyed in the pit they dug for me.
NLT.9:
Then I will rejoice in the LORD.
I will be glad because he rescues me.
NLT.10:
With every bone in my body I will praise him:
“LORD, who can compare with you?
Who else rescues the helpless from the strong?
Who else protects the helpless and poor from those who rob them?”
NLT.11:
Malicious witnesses testify against me.
They accuse me of crimes I know nothing about.
NLT.12:
They repay me evil for good.
I am sick with despair.
NLT.13:
Yet when they were ill, I grieved for them.
I denied myself by fasting for them,
but my prayers returned unanswered.
NLT.14:
I was sad, as though they were my friends or family,
as if I were grieving for my own mother.
NLT.15:
But they are glad now that I am in trouble;
they gleefully join together against me.
I am attacked by people I don’t even know;
they slander me constantly.
NLT.16:
They mock me and call me names;
they snarl at me.
NLT.17:
How long, O Lord, will you look on and do nothing?
Rescue me from their fierce attacks.
Protect my life from these lions!
NLT.18:
Then I will thank you in front of the great assembly.
I will praise you before all the people.
NLT.19:
Don’t let my treacherous enemies rejoice over my defeat.
Don’t let those who hate me without cause gloat over my sorrow.
NLT.20:
They don’t talk of peace;
they plot against innocent people who mind their own business.
NLT.21:
They shout, “Aha! Aha!
With our own eyes we saw him do it!”
NLT.22:
O LORD, you know all about this.
Do not stay silent.
Do not abandon me now, O Lord.
NLT.23:
Wake up! Rise to my defense!
Take up my case, my God and my Lord.
NLT.24:
Declare me not guilty, O LORD my God, for you give justice.
Don’t let my enemies laugh about me in my troubles.
NLT.25:
Don’t let them say, “Look, we got what we wanted!
Now we will eat him alive!”
NLT.26:
May those who rejoice at my troubles
be humiliated and disgraced.
May those who triumph over me
be covered with shame and dishonor.
NLT.27:
But give great joy to those who came to my defense.
Let them continually say, “Great is the LORD,
who delights in blessing his servant with peace!”
NLT.28:
Then I will proclaim your justice,
and I will praise you all day long.

WEB.1: By David. Contend, Yahweh, with those who contend with me. Fight against those who fight against me.
WEB.2: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for my help.
WEB.3: Brandish the spear and block those who pursue me. Tell my soul, “I am your salvation.”
WEB.4: Let those who seek after my soul be disappointed and brought to dishonor. Let those who plot my ruin be turned back and confounded.
WEB.5: Let them be as chaff before the wind, Yahweh’s angel driving them on.
WEB.6: Let their way be dark and slippery, Yahweh’s angel pursuing them.
WEB.7: For without cause they have hidden their net in a pit for me. Without cause they have dug a pit for my soul.
WEB.8: Let destruction come on him unawares. Let his net that he has hidden catch himself. Let him fall into that destruction.
WEB.9: My soul shall be joyful in Yahweh. It shall rejoice in his salvation.
WEB.10: All my bones shall say, “Yahweh, who is like you, who delivers the poor from him who is too strong for him; yes, the poor and the needy from him who robs him?”
WEB.11: Unrighteous witnesses rise up. They ask me about things that I don’t know about.
WEB.12: They reward me evil for good, to the bereaving of my soul.
WEB.13: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth. I afflicted my soul with fasting. My prayer returned into my own bosom.
WEB.14: I behaved myself as though it had been my friend or my brother. I bowed down mourning, as one who mourns his mother.
WEB.15: But in my adversity, they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together. The attackers gathered themselves together against me, and I didn’t know it. They tore at me, and didn’t cease.
WEB.16: Like the profane mockers in feasts, they gnashed their teeth at me.
WEB.17: Lord, how long will you look on? Rescue my soul from their destruction, my precious life from the lions.
WEB.18: I will give you thanks in the great assembly. I will praise you among many people.
WEB.19: Don’t let those who are my enemies wrongfully rejoice over me; neither let those who hate me without a cause wink their eyes.
WEB.20: For they don’t speak peace, but they devise deceitful words against those who are quiet in the land.
WEB.21: Yes, they opened their mouth wide against me. They said, “Aha! Aha! Our eye has seen it!”
WEB.22: You have seen it, Yahweh. Don’t keep silent. Lord, don’t be far from me.
WEB.23: Wake up! Rise up to defend me, my God! My Lord, contend for me!
WEB.24: Vindicate me, Yahweh my God, according to your righteousness. Don’t let them gloat over me.
WEB.25: Don’t let them say in their heart, “Aha! That’s the way we want it!” Don’t let them say, “We have swallowed him up!”
WEB.26: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who rejoice at my calamity. Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me.
WEB.27: Let them shout for joy and be glad, who favor my righteous cause. Yes, let them say continually, “Yahweh be magnified, who has pleasure in the prosperity of his servant!”
WEB.28: My tongue shall talk about your righteousness and about your praise all day long.

CSB17.1:
Of David. [1] Oppose my opponents, LORD;
fight those who fight me. ⓐ
CSB17.2:
Take your shields — large and small —
and come to my aid. ⓑ
CSB17.3:
Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers,
and assure me: “I am your deliverance.” ⓒ
CSB17.4:
Let those who intend to take my life
be disgraced and humiliated;
let those who plan to harm me
be turned back and ashamed. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
Let them be like chaff in the wind,
with the angel of the LORD driving them away. ⓔ
CSB17.6:
Let their way be dark and slippery,
with the angel of the LORD pursuing them. ⓕ
CSB17.7:
They hid their net for me without cause;
they dug a pit for me without cause. ⓖ
CSB17.8:
Let ruin come on him unexpectedly,
and let the net that he hid ensnare him;
let him fall into it — to his ruin. ⓗ
CSB17.9:
Then I will rejoice in the LORD;
I will delight in his deliverance. ⓘ
CSB17.10:
All my bones will say,
“LORD, who is like you, ⓙ
rescuing the poor from one too strong for him,
the poor or the needy from one who robs him?” ⓚ
CSB17.11:
Malicious witnesses come forward;
they question me about things I do not know. ⓛ
CSB17.12:
They repay me evil for good,
making me desolate. ⓜ
CSB17.13:
Yet when they were sick,
my clothing was sackcloth;
I humbled myself with fasting,
and my prayer was genuine. [1] ⓝ
CSB17.14:
I went about mourning as if for my friend or brother;
I was bowed down with grief,
like one mourning for a mother. ⓐ
CSB17.15:
But when I stumbled, they gathered in glee;
they gathered against me.
Assailants I did not know
tore at me and did not stop. ⓑ
CSB17.16:
With godless mockery [2]
they gnashed their teeth at me. ⓒ
CSB17.17:
Lord, how long will you look on? ⓓ
Rescue me from their ravages;
rescue my precious life from the young lions. ⓔ
CSB17.18:
I will praise you in the great assembly;
I will exalt you among many people. ⓕ
CSB17.19:
Do not let my deceitful enemies rejoice over me;
do not let those who hate me without cause ⓖ
wink at me maliciously. ⓗ
CSB17.20:
For they do not speak in friendly ways,
but contrive fraudulent schemes [3]
against those who live peacefully in the land. ⓘ
CSB17.21:
They open their mouths wide against me and say,
“Aha, aha! We saw it!” [4] ⓙ
CSB17.22:
You saw it, LORD; do not be silent.
Lord, do not be far from me. ⓚ
CSB17.23:
Wake up and rise to my defense,
to my cause, my God and my Lord! ⓛ
CSB17.24:
Vindicate me, LORD my God,
in keeping with your righteousness,
and do not let them rejoice over me. ⓜ
CSB17.25:
Do not let them say in their hearts,
“Aha! Just what we wanted.”
Do not let them say,
“We have swallowed him up!” ⓝ
CSB17.26:
Let those who rejoice at my misfortune
be disgraced and humiliated;
let those who exalt themselves over me
be clothed with shame and reproach. ⓐ
CSB17.27:
Let those who want my vindication
shout for joy and be glad;
let them continually say,
“The LORD be exalted.
He takes pleasure in his servant’s well-being.” ⓑ
CSB17.28:
And my tongue will proclaim your righteousness,
your praise all day long. ⓒ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me;
Fight against those who fight against me.
AMP.2:
Take hold of shield and buckler ( small shield),
And stand up for my help.
AMP.3:
Draw also the spear and javelin to meet those who pursue me.
Say to my soul, "I am your salvation."
AMP.4:
Let those be ashamed and dishonored who seek my life;
Let those be turned back [in defeat] and humiliated who plot evil against me.
AMP.5:
Let them be [blown away] like chaff before the wind [worthless, without substance],
With the angel of the LORD driving them on.
AMP.6:
Let their way be dark and slippery,
With the angel of the LORD pursuing and harassing them.
AMP.7:
For without cause they hid their net for me;
Without cause they dug a pit [of destruction] for my life.
AMP.8:
Let destruction come upon my enemy by surprise;
Let the net he hid for me catch him;
Into that very destruction let him fall.
AMP.9:
Then my soul shall rejoice in the LORD;
It shall rejoice in His salvation.
AMP.10:
All my bones will say, "LORD, who is like You,
Who rescues the afflicted from him who is too strong for him [to resist alone],
And the afflicted and the needy from him who robs him?"
AMP.11:
Malicious witnesses rise up;
They ask me of things that I do not know.
AMP.12:
They repay me evil for good,
To the sorrow of my soul.
AMP.13:
But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth (mourning garment);
I humbled my soul with fasting,
And I prayed with my head bowed on my chest.
AMP.14:
I behaved as if grieving for my friend or my brother;
I bowed down in mourning, as one who sorrows for his mother.
AMP.15:
But in my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered together [against me];
The slanderers whom I did not know gathered against me;
They slandered and reviled me without ceasing.
AMP.16:
Like godless jesters at a feast,
They gnashed at me with their teeth [in malice].
AMP.17:
LORD, how long will You look on [without action]?
Rescue my life from their destructions,
My only life from the young lions.
AMP.18:
I will give You thanks in the great congregation;
I will praise You among a mighty people.
AMP.19:
Do not let those who are wrongfully my enemies rejoice over me;
Nor let those who hate me without cause wink their eye [maliciously].
AMP.20:
For they do not speak peace,
But they devise deceitful words [half-truths and lies] against those who are quiet in the land.
AMP.21:
They open their mouths wide against me;
They say, "Aha, aha, our eyes have seen it!"
AMP.22:
You have seen this, O LORD; do not keep silent.
O Lord, do not be far from me.
AMP.23:
Wake Yourself up, and arise to my right
And to my cause, my God and my Lord.
AMP.24:
Judge me, O LORD my God, according to Your righteousness and justice;
And do not let them rejoice over me.
AMP.25:
Do not let them say in their heart, "Aha, that is what we wanted!"
Do not let them say, "We have swallowed him up and destroyed him."
AMP.26:
Let those be ashamed and humiliated together who rejoice at my distress;
Let those be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves over me.
AMP.27:
Let them shout for joy and rejoice, who favor my vindication and want what is right for me;
Let them say continually, "Let the LORD be magnified, who delights and takes pleasure in the prosperity of His servant."
AMP.28:
And my tongue shall declare Your righteousness (justice),
And Your praise all the day long.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Harass these hecklers, God,
punch these bullies in the nose.
MSG.2:
Grab a weapon, anything at hand;
stand up for me!
MSG.3:
Get ready to throw the spear, aim the javelin,
at the people who are out to get me.
Reassure me; let me hear you say,
"I'll save you."
MSG.4:
When those thugs try to knife me in the back,
make them look foolish.
Frustrate all those
who are plotting my downfall.
MSG.5:
Make them like cinders in a high wind,
with God's angel working the bellows.
MSG.6:
Make their road lightless and mud-slick,
with God's angel on their tails.
MSG.7:
Out of sheer cussedness they set a trap to catch me;
for no good reason they dug a ditch to stop me.
MSG.8:
Surprise them with your ambush—
catch them in the very trap they set,
the disaster they planned for me.
MSG.9:
But let me run loose and free,
celebrating God's great work,
MSG.10:
Every bone in my body laughing, singing, "God,
there's no one like you.
You put the down-and-out on their feet
and protect the unprotected from bullies!"
MSG.11:
Hostile accusers appear out of nowhere,
they stand up and badger me.
MSG.12:
They pay me back misery for mercy,
leaving my soul empty.
MSG.13:
When they were sick, I dressed in black;
instead of eating, I prayed.
MSG.14:
My prayers were like lead in my gut,
like I'd lost my best friend, my brother.
I paced, distraught as a motherless child,
hunched and heavyhearted.
MSG.15:
But when I was down
they threw a party!
All the nameless riffraff of the town came
chanting insults about me.
MSG.16:
Like barbarians desecrating a shrine,
they destroyed my reputation.
MSG.17:
God, how long are you going
to stand there doing nothing?
Save me from their brutalities;
everything I've got is being thrown to the lions.
MSG.18:
I will give you full credit
when everyone gathers for worship;
When the people turn out in force
I will say my Hallelujahs.
MSG.19:
Don't let these liars, my enemies,
have a party at my expense,
Those who hate me for no reason,
winking and rolling their eyes.
MSG.20:
No good is going to come
from that crowd;
They spend all their time cooking up gossip
against those who mind their own business.
MSG.21:
They open their mouths
in ugly grins,
Mocking, "Ha-ha, ha-ha, thought you'd get away with it?
We've caught you hands down!"
MSG.22:
Don't you see what they're doing, God?
You're not going to let them
Get by with it, are you? Not going to walk off
without doing something, are you?
MSG.23:
Please get up—wake up! Tend to my case.
My God, my Lord—my life is on the line.
MSG.24:
Do what you think is right, God, my God,
but don't make me pay for their good time.
MSG.25:
Don't let them say to themselves,
"Ha-ha, we got what we wanted."
Don't let them say,
"We've chewed him up and spit him out."
MSG.26:
Let those who are being hilarious
at my expense
Be made to look ridiculous.
Make them wear donkey's ears;
Pin them with the donkey's tail,
who made themselves so high and mighty!
MSG.27:
But those who want
the best for me,
Let them have the last word—a glad shout!—
and say, over and over and over,
"God is great—everything works
together for good for his servant."
MSG.28:
I'll tell the world how great and good you are,
I'll shout Hallelujah all day, every day.

NIV.1:
Psalm 35
Of David.
Contend, O Lord, with those who contend with me;
fight against those who fight against me.
NIV.2:
Take up shield and buckler;
arise and come to my aid.
NIV.3:
Brandish spear and javelin
against those who pursue me.
Say to my soul,
“I am your salvation.”
NIV.4:
May those who seek my life
be disgraced and put to shame;
may those who plot my ruin
be turned back in dismay.
NIV.5:
May they be like chaff before the wind,
with the angel of the Lord driving them away;
NIV.6:
may their path be dark and slippery,
with the angel of the Lord pursuing them.
NIV.7:
Since they hid their net for me without cause
and without cause dug a pit for me,
NIV.8:
may ruin overtake them by surprise—
may the net they hid entangle them,
may they fall into the pit, to their ruin.
NIV.9:
Then my soul will rejoice in the Lord
and delight in his salvation.
NIV.10:
My whole being will exclaim,
“Who is like you, O Lord?
You rescue the poor from those too strong for them,
the poor and needy from those who rob them.”
NIV.11:
Ruthless witnesses come forward;
they question me on things I know nothing about.
NIV.12:
They repay me evil for good
and leave my soul forlorn.
NIV.13:
Yet when they were ill, I put on sackcloth
and humbled myself with fasting.
When my prayers returned to me unanswered,
NIV.14:
I went about mourning
as though for my friend or brother.
I bowed my head in grief
as though weeping for my mother.
NIV.15:
But when I stumbled, they gathered in glee;
attackers gathered against me when I was unaware.
They slandered me without ceasing.
NIV.16:
Like the ungodly they maliciously mocked;
they gnashed their teeth at me.
NIV.17:
O Lord, how long will you look on?
Rescue my life from their ravages,
my precious life from these lions.
NIV.18:
I will give you thanks in the great assembly;
among throngs of people I will praise you.
NIV.19:
Let not those gloat over me
who are my enemies without cause;
let not those who hate me without reason
maliciously wink the eye.
NIV.20:
They do not speak peaceably,
but devise false accusations
against those who live quietly in the land.
NIV.21:
They gape at me and say, “Aha! Aha!
With our own eyes we have seen it.”
NIV.22:
O Lord, you have seen this; be not silent.
Do not be far from me, O Lord.
NIV.23:
Awake, and rise to my defense!
Contend for me, my God and Lord.
NIV.24:
Vindicate me in your righteousness, O Lord my God;
do not let them gloat over me.
NIV.25:
Do not let them think, “Aha, just what we wanted!”
or say, “We have swallowed him up.”
NIV.26:
May all who gloat over my distress
be put to shame and confusion;
may all who exalt themselves over me
be clothed with shame and disgrace.
NIV.27:
May those who delight in my vindication
shout for joy and gladness;
may they always say, “The Lord be exalted,
who delights in the well-being of his servant.”
NIV.28:
My tongue will speak of your righteousness
and of your praises all day long.

NRSVCE.1:
Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me;
fight against those who fight against me!
NRSVCE.2:
Take hold of shield and buckler,
and rise up to help me!
NRSVCE.3:
Draw the spear and javelin
against my pursuers;
say to my soul,
“I am your salvation.”
NRSVCE.4:
Let them be put to shame and dishonor
who seek after my life.
Let them be turned back and confounded
who devise evil against me.
NRSVCE.5:
Let them be like chaff before the wind,
with the angel of the LORD driving them on.
NRSVCE.6:
Let their way be dark and slippery,
with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
NRSVCE.7:
For without cause they hid their net for me;
without cause they dug a pit for my life.
NRSVCE.8:
Let ruin come on them unawares.
And let the net that they hid ensnare them;
let them fall in it — to their ruin.
NRSVCE.9:
Then my soul shall rejoice in the LORD,
exulting in his deliverance.
NRSVCE.10:
All my bones shall say,
“O LORD, who is like you?
You deliver the weak
from those too strong for them,
the weak and needy from those who despoil them.”
NRSVCE.11:
Malicious witnesses rise up;
they ask me about things I do not know.
NRSVCE.12:
They repay me evil for good;
my soul is forlorn.
NRSVCE.13:
But as for me, when they were sick,
I wore sackcloth;
I afflicted myself with fasting.
I prayed with head bowed on my bosom,
NRSVCE.14:
as though I grieved for a friend or a brother;
I went about as one who laments for a mother,
bowed down and in mourning.
NRSVCE.15:
But at my stumbling they gathered in glee,
they gathered together against me;
ruffians whom I did not know
tore at me without ceasing;
NRSVCE.16:
they impiously mocked more and more,
gnashing at me with their teeth.
NRSVCE.17:
How long, O LORD, will you look on?
Rescue me from their ravages,
my life from the lions!
NRSVCE.18:
Then I will thank you in the great congregation;
in the mighty throng I will praise you.
NRSVCE.19:
Do not let my treacherous enemies rejoice over me,
or those who hate me without cause wink the eye.
NRSVCE.20:
For they do not speak peace,
but they conceive deceitful words
against those who are quiet in the land.
NRSVCE.21:
They open wide their mouths against me;
they say, “Aha, Aha,
our eyes have seen it.”
NRSVCE.22:
You have seen, O LORD; do not be silent!
O Lord, do not be far from me!
NRSVCE.23:
Wake up! Bestir yourself for my defense,
for my cause, my God and my Lord!
NRSVCE.24:
Vindicate me, O LORD, my God,
according to your righteousness,
and do not let them rejoice over me.
NRSVCE.25:
Do not let them say to themselves,
“Aha, we have our heart’s desire.”
Do not let them say, “We have swallowed you up.”
NRSVCE.26:
Let all those who rejoice at my calamity
be put to shame and confusion;
let those who exalt themselves against me
be clothed with shame and dishonor.
NRSVCE.27:
Let those who desire my vindication
shout for joy and be glad,
and say evermore,
“Great is the LORD,
who delights in the welfare of his servant.”
NRSVCE.28:
Then my tongue shall tell of your righteousness
and of your praise all day long.

NET.1: O LORD, fight those who fight with me! Attack those who attack me!
NET.2: Grab your small shield and large shield, and rise up to help me!
NET.3: Use your spear and lance against those who chase me! Assure me with these words: “I am your deliverer!”
NET.4: May those who seek my life be embarrassed and humiliated! May those who plan to harm me be turned back and ashamed!
NET.5: May they be like wind-driven chaff, as the LORD’s angel attacks them!
NET.6: May their path be dark and slippery, as the LORD’s angel chases them!
NET.7: I did not harm them, but they hid a net to catch me and dug a pit to trap me.
NET.8: Let destruction take them by surprise! Let the net they hid catch them! Let them fall into destruction!
NET.9: Then I will rejoice in the LORD and be happy because of his deliverance.
NET.10: With all my strength I will say, “O LORD, who can compare to you? You rescue the oppressed from those who try to overpower them; the oppressed and needy from those who try to rob them.”
NET.11: Violent men perjure themselves, and falsely accuse me.
NET.12: They repay me evil for the good I have done; I am overwhelmed with sorrow.
NET.13: When they were sick, I wore sackcloth, and refrained from eating food. (If I am lying, may my prayers go unanswered!)
NET.14: I mourned for them as I would for a friend or my brother. I bowed down in sorrow as if I were mourning for my mother.
NET.15: But when I stumbled, they rejoiced and gathered together; they gathered together to ambush me. They tore at me without stopping to rest.
NET.16: When I tripped, they taunted me relentlessly, and tried to bite me.
NET.17: O Lord, how long are you going to just stand there and watch this? Rescue me from their destructive attacks; guard my life from the young lions!
NET.18: Then I will give you thanks in the great assembly; I will praise you before a large crowd of people!
NET.19: Do not let those who are my enemies for no reason gloat over me! Do not let those who hate me without cause carry out their wicked schemes!
NET.20: For they do not try to make peace with others, but plan ways to deceive those who are unsuspecting.
NET.21: They are ready to devour me; they say, “Aha! Aha! We’ve got you!”
NET.22: But you take notice, LORD! O Lord, do not remain far away from me!
NET.23: Rouse yourself, wake up and vindicate me! My God and Lord, defend my just cause!
NET.24: Vindicate me by your justice, O LORD my God! Do not let them gloat over me!
NET.25: Do not let them say to themselves, “Aha! We have what we wanted!” Do not let them say, “We have devoured him!”
NET.26: May those who want to harm me be totally embarrassed and ashamed! May those who arrogantly taunt me be covered with shame and humiliation!
NET.27: May those who desire my vindication shout for joy and rejoice! May they continually say, “May the LORD be praised, for he wants his servant to be secure.”
NET.28: Then I will tell others about your justice, and praise you all day long.
